que: What is the main purpose of setState() in Flutter?
options: To notify Flutter that local state changed and the UI should rebuild
To save data to shared preferences
Inside the State class of a StatefulWidget

que: What should be wrapped inside setState()?
Which type of state is setState() best suited for?
What happens after setState() is called?
Which practice is recommended when using setState()?
options: Put long-running async work inside setState()
Call setState() in every build method
Use setState() for all app-wide state

que: What should you avoid doing with setState()?
options: Calling it after the widget is disposed

que: Why might you check mounted before calling setState()?
options: To confirm the widget is still in the tree
